pattern: Walk the space-separated tokens once, tracking only the previous number seen, since the numbers in a sentence are ascending exactly when each new one strictly exceeds the last.
hints: Split the sentence on spaces and look only at the tokens made entirely of digits -- skip every word untouched.
Keep a single running 'previous number seen' value (start it below any possible number) and compare each new number against it.
The moment a number fails to be strictly greater than the previous one, return false; if you finish the scan without that happening, return true.

edge_inputs.description: exactly two numbers, equal to each other
numbers near the maximum two-digit bound
minimal sentence made of just the two required numbers
numbers separated by several words, decreasing later in the sentence
a large ascending jump followed by a small final drop
input: count 1 grows to 100 then falls to 2

edge_inputs.description: several single-digit numbers, all strictly ascending

stress.description: randomly interleaved words and numbers up to the real length limit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    tokens = []
    cur_len = 0
    limit = 200
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    while True:
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            tok = str(rng.randint(1, 99))
        else:
            wlen = rng.randint(1, 5)
            tok = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(wlen))
        add_len = len(tok) + (1 if tokens else 0)
        if cur_len + add_len > limit - 1 or len(tokens) >= 60:
            break
        tokens.append(tok)
        cur_len += add_len
    num_count = sum(1 for t in tokens if t.isdigit())
    while num_count < 2:
        tokens.append(str(rng.randint(1, 99)))
        num_count += 1
    s = ' '.join(tokens)
    if len(s) < 3:
        s = 'a 1 b 2'
    return [s]
